title: Introduction to Accounting and Financial Reporting
catalogDescription: Nature and social setting of accounting; uses of accounting information; introduction of basic accounting concepts and procedures; interpretation of financial statements. May not be repeated. Maximum 15 credits allowed between ACCTG 215; ACCTG 225; T ACCT 210; T ACCT 220; and T ACCT 230 (or equivalents). Course equivalent to: B BUS 210.

title: Essentials of Accounting
catalogDescription: Provides an introduction to basic accounting principles and procedures; use of accounting information to make decisions from the perspective of both external investors and internal managers. Cannot be taken for credit if credit has already been earned for ACCTG 225.

title: Fundamentals of Managerial Accounting
catalogDescription: Covers application of basic costing concepts and tools for planning, control, and strategic decision making. Concentrates on information useful to enterprise managers. May not be repeated. Maximum 15 credits allowed between ACCTG 215; ACCTG 225; T ACCT 210; T ACCT 220; and T ACCT 230 (or equivalents). Course equivalent to: B BUS 211. Prerequisite: ACCTG 215 and ECON 200.
